x-enumeration-literals.name: Pro-Rata Allocation Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is distributed proportionally to the size of each obligation or claim.

Comment:
Example:

Total collateral available: $100,000

Party As obligation: $60,000

Party Bs obligation: $40,000

Allocation: Party A gets $60,000 × 100,000 ÷ 100,000 = $60,000, Party B gets $40,000

x-enumeration-literals.name: First-Come, First-Served Allocation Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is allocated in the order requests are received until collateral runs out.

Comment:
Example:

Total collateral: $50,000

Party A requests $30,000 → allocated $30,000

Party B requests $30,000 → only $20,000 left → allocated $20,000

x-enumeration-literals.name: Risk-Based Allocation Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is assigned based on the risk profile of obligations or counterparties; higher-risk exposures get priority.

Comment:
Example:

Party A (high risk) and Party B (low risk)

Collateral available: $100,000

Party As obligation: $70,000 → allocated $70,000 first

Party B receives remaining $30,000

x-enumeration-literals.name: Fixed Allocation Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specific collateral assets are pre-designated for particular obligations or counterparties.

Comment:
Example:

Collateral pool: Bonds X, Y, Z

Party A is assigned Bond X

Party B is assigned Bond Y

Party C is assigned Bond Z

x-enumeration-literals.name: Conditional Allocation Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is allocated only if certain conditions or thresholds are met.

Comment:
Example:

Collateral is allocated to Party A only if their exposure exceeds $50,000

Party As current exposure: $45,000 → no allocation

Exposure increases to $55,000 → collateral allocated

x-enumeration-literals.name: Hierarchical Allocation Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is allocated according to a hierarchy of obligations, often by seniority or contractual rank.
Purpose: Protects senior obligations before subordinated ones.

Comment:
Example:

Senior debt: Party A → $60,000

Subordinated debt: Party B → $40,000

Total collateral: $70,000

Allocation: Party A gets $60,000 (full), Party B gets remaining $10,000

x-enumeration-literals.name: Priority-Based Allocation Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is assigned according to a predefined ranking order.

Comment:
Example:
Loan A (priority 1): €60k
Loan B (priority 2): €40k
Collateral: €70k

x-enumeration-literals.name: Full Coverage Allocation Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is assigned to fully cover a specific obligation before any other allocation occurs.
Focuses on complete coverage of a selected exposure
Often used for secured or critical loans

Comment:
Example:
Loan A requires full coverage: €50k
Loan B: €50k
Collateral: €50k
